item.title: France's government is ditching Windows for Linux, calling US tech dependence a strategic risk
item.description: The ministries have until the fall to find a solution.
item.author: (Simon Batt)

item.title: Installing every* Firefox extension
item.description: Oh, you use Firefox? Name every extension.

item.title: linux/Documentation/process/coding-assistants.rst at master · torvalds/linux
item.description: Linux kernel source tree. Contribute to torvalds/linux development by creating an account on GitHub.

item.title: Your friends are hiding their best ideas from you
item.description: Back in college, the final project in our JavaScript class was to build a website. We were a group of four, and we built the best website in class. It was for a restaurant called the Coral Reef. We fo

item.title: On filing the corners off my MacBooks
item.description: April 2026

item.title: Claude Code is a vibe-coded mess. Some of it is actually good.
item.description: Some ideas worth stealing from the leaked Claude Code source: deferred tool loading, diminishing returns detection, cache-aware context compaction, and coalesced background extraction.

item.title: I Already Lost This Once
item.description: AI is stripping meaning from developers' careers. My body stripped it from mine years ago. I've been where they are, and the craft survived.

item.title: I shipped a transaction bug, so I built a linter
item.description: Database operations leaked outside a transaction and broke prod. Here's how I built a Go linter to prevent it.

item.title: The anatomy of DESIGN.md | TypeUI
item.description: Learn more about the design.md file and how to integrate these design skills into your project and leverage AI when building user interfaces
item.author: (Zoltan Szogyenyi)

item.title: ~fpereiro
item.description: A number of programmers, me included, feel that sometime between 2025 and 2026, we lost our craft. We used to write our own code by hand. The arrival of powerful LLM models and coding agents built upon them has shifted us from writers of code to managers and reviewers of agents that produce code.

item.title: Make Your Phone a Tool ∙ Daniel Fernandes
item.description: These steps are tailored to Android devices. The ideas can adapt to iOS.

item.title: Why aren't we uv yet?
item.description: A look at what the hottest new Python repos are doing in 2026

item.title: Why I'm Building a Database Engine in C#
item.description: Everyone says you need C, C++, or Rust for a high-performance database engine. I chose C# — here's why that's not as crazy as it sounds.

item.title: No-stack web development
item.description: The one where I code artisanal organic free-range websites

item.title: Deconstructing a Black Hole
item.description: An interactive deep dive into the WebGL fragment shader behind a procedural black hole animation. Simplex noise, domain warping, and GLSL, explained from first principles.

item.title: A Type System for Management
item.description: In my book Rusty Engineering Management I meander to a point I make explicitly here: There is a type system for engineering management and all you need to understand it is Rust’s borrow-checker.

item.title: Mind Mind Game Game
item.description: There is another element which is missing, or drawn into the other two: games of mind. "Bluff" would be another term, or "second-guessing". Poker is chance, but the real game is convincing other players what chance has befallen you -- and this may have nothing to do with the cards in your hand. Even chess, with no hidden information, may hide traps and feints: the mechanics are complex enough that a player can conceal his strategy in a maze of future possibility.

item.title: Mapping my reading log by author birthplace
item.description: Visualizing a personal reading log on a world map, plotting each book by its author's birthplace.

item.title: Rolling your own crypto can make sense (sometimes)
item.description: A couple of months ago, I took on the endeavour to add support for the Noise handshake into Quincy, for easier user identity handling and some anti-detection benefits. This took me down the rabbit hol…

item.title: Are digital twin frameworks following good coding practices?
item.description: Digital twin is a virtual copy of a physical system or object that helps to understand and analyze the behavior of the system. It uses real-time data from sensors, IoT devices, and digital input devices in order to monitor, analyse, visualise, and simulate the behaviour of the systems. Engineers can

item.description: Settling for good enough is not good enough.

item.title: GitHub - Keychron/Keychron-Keyboards-Hardware-Design: Industrial design files for Keychron keyboards and mice. 100+ models with CAD assets in STEP, DXF, DWG, and PDF. Source-available, with commercial use allowed for original compatible accessories within the license terms.

item.title: Lightning
[ANNOUNCE] WireGuardNT v0.11 and WireGuard for Windows v0.6 Released
1D-Chess
item.description: 1d-chess is a new variant where you can play the beautiful game without all those unneccessary and complicated extra dimensions. Play as white against the AI. You might initally find it more difficult than expected, but assming optimal play, is there a forced win for white?

item.title: Why you can’t trust Privacy & Security
item.description: How to gain access to the contents of privacy-protected folders even though Privacy & Security settings say that access is denied.

item.title: The AWS Lambda 'Kiss of Death' - Shattered Silicon
item.description: Our story begins as most database issues start: with hands on foreheads, internally or externally, saying ‘WTF is going on?’. We observed a series of database freezes on our production environment. It was quite severe. Connections spiked, writes were stalled and at some point, a large database freeze and they cleared. Being a Galera environment, […]
